extracted_information: A content delivery network (CDN) is a globally distributed network of servers designed to optimize the delivery of web content to users. By caching content closer to the user, CDNs improve the speed and reliability of web services. This article traces the evolution of CDN technology from its origins in static content delivery to sophisticated edge networks, exploring innovations in response to changing online content delivery landscapes.

CDN_Generations_Overview.Context: Internet's earliest days with text-only static HTML, growing complexity led to 'World Wide Wait'.
CDN_Generations_Overview.Focus: Caching static content (images, videos, HTML files) on distributed servers.
CDN_Generations_Overview.Benefits: Minimized round-trip time (latency), improved load times, alleviated congestion on origin servers, improved user experience.

CDN_Generations_Overview.Context: Surge in demand for dynamic/interactive content, migration to faster broadband, rise of on-demand and live video streaming (YouTube, Netflix, Hulu, Amazon Prime Video), mobile internet access, Web 2.0 (user-generated content).
CDN_Generations_Overview.Focus: Expanded capabilities to support dynamic content delivery.
CDN_Generations_Overview.Capabilities: Database-driven websites, personalized content, social media, streaming services.

CDN_Generations_Overview.Context: Intensive increase in web users (3 billion by 2015), increased cyber threats, exponential e-commerce growth (online payments, user credentials).
CDN_Generations_Overview.Capabilities: SSL/TLS encryption, web application firewalls (WAF), DDoS protection. Act as reverse proxies to mitigate threats.

CDN_Generations_Overview.Concept: Leverages edge computing, bringing processing power closer to the network edge. Highly distributed infrastructure in diverse geographic locations.
CDN_Generations_Overview.Benefits: Significantly reduces latency, enables faster response times, enhances user experience for dynamic/interactive applications. Enhanced security and data privacy, rich media streaming, content optimization.
Key_Technologies_and_Strategies: Serverless architectures: Deploy and run code without managing servers, facilitating scalable and cost-effective application deployment.
AI integration: Optimizes content delivery, predicts user behavior, and mitigates security risks in real time. Enables efficient resource allocation and proactive threat detection.

CDN_Generational_Comparison_Table.Feature: Basic network security (L3, L4 DDoS protection)
Edge computing (AI inference at the edge)

Gcore_Specific_Features_and_Offerings: Robust CDN enhancing static and dynamic content delivery.
Powerful image optimization designed for web optimization tasks.
Built-in web security including L3/L4/L7 DDoS protection.
FastEdge: WebAssembly-based serverless edge computing product that runs on their CDN, designed for sophisticated workloads.
WAAP solution bolstered by recent acquisition.

statistics.Internet_Users_2015: Approximately 3 billion (a 50% growth in just five years).

geographical_data.Edge_Network_Infrastructure: Highly distributed infrastructure, deployed in diverse geographic locations.
